questions.prompt: In Printix, which of these statements about the Site / Network model is correct?
choices: A Network can belong to multiple Sites for redundancy
A Network belongs to exactly one Site, and printers belong to the Site of their Network
Sites are obsolete and only Networks are used
A Folder can only contain one Site
questions.explanation: The model is one-to-one: a Network belongs to one Site; printers inherit the Site from their Network. Folders are containers for grouping Sites geographically.

questions.prompt: Site folder hierarchies in Printix can go up to four levels deep.
questions.explanation: Folders can be nested up to four levels, useful for region / country / city / building structures in larger customers. Most SMB customers stay flat.
